title: Allign container image storage configuration with Buildah
body: vfs storage driver is often used in environments where enabling fuse is not possible. Buildah images already take this into account. This PR basically adds same config as existing Buildah images, see https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/contrib/buildahimage/stable/Dockerfile#L20.

comments.body:
LGTM

@Fodoj Thanks

You need to sign your commits.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

The changes that you have LGTM, however you missed this file:  https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/contrib/podmanimage/stable/manual/Containerfile.

Could you do the same there please and thanks!
@Fodoj Please Rebase this PR so that tests will pass.
@TomSweeneyRedHat added the changed. Just curious - why keep using Dockerfile instead of Containerfile in this repo?

@Fodoj Sadly what @rhatdan said.  We use quay.io's image building mechanism to generate the images when a PR merges into the master branch.  That mechanism lets you select the directory where the Dockerfile lives, but the file must be named Dockerfile.  We've put in an RFE with quay.io to allow any file name, or at least Containerfile, but that may take a while to happen.
